resolution_status_highlights.C-1_flag_file_ownership_unregistered: fixed_as_specified — 2 new files_to_modify (build.js OWNERSHIP + workspace-sync.md OWNED_BY_GIT), both with dry_run_check asserting current absence (idempotent).
resolution_status_highlights.C-2_shadow_mode_skips_consumption_tracki: fixed_differently — argued in disagreements_with_review with a concrete worked example (qid 100 + qid 101 soft-match race) that the current code already writes popped_by_queue_id for real matches regardless of enforce/shadow. Added an explicit INVARIANT doc comment in the Step E2 bash preamble so a future editor cannot accidentally wrap the real-match claim path in an enforce gate.
resolution_status_highlights.C-3_cycle_numbering_mtime_backwards: fixed_as_specified — re-statted SEC-4.1.c3 (mtime 00:16:21Z) and c4 (mtime 06:12:46Z) via the FUSE workspace. c3 was written ~5h56m BEFORE c4 — consistent with its lower cycle number, NOT a cycle-ordering violation. The real anomaly is c3's internal reviewed_at=07:42Z being 7h26m AFTER its own mtime. Rewrote orthogonal_finding and the seeded ISS description to match.
resolution_status_highlights.block_7_shell_quote_bug: fixed_as_specified — double-quoted shell form: grep -Fq -- "### 7b. Validate the JSON (mandatory)" monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md && grep -Fq -- "console.log('valid')" monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md. Executed end-to-end against clone, exits 0.
resolution_status_highlights.Mj-1_validator_trigger_guard: fixed_as_specified — enumerated known trigger values in a Set, defaults to WARN for unknown triggers, annotated <filename>.json as a literal placeholder requiring substitution with a note above the code block.
resolution_status_highlights.Mj-2_shadow_log_dedupe: fixed_as_specified — block 1 pre-loads existing shadow log tuples (queue_id, pushed_at) and skips repeat logging, keeping the ≤3/day threshold a count of distinct stuck items.
resolution_status_highlights.Mj-3_C1_evidence_side_by_side: fixed_as_specified — added byte-level evidence to disagreements_with_review.C-1_escape_correctness quoting raw JSON bytes (char codes 92, 92, 100, 43), JSON-decoded result (1 backslash + d+), and decider.md bytes at the same region (1 backslash + d+). content.includes(find) returns TRUE. Hostile #1 C-1 was factually wrong.
resolution_status_highlights.Mj-4_audit_flip_criterion_reframe: fixed_as_specified — denominator is NEW reviews (reviewed_at > step-1 commit time); target ≥80% of NEW queue-sourced reviews carry queue_id; rolling 3-day window must show strict_queue_id MAJORITY (>50%) of all pop_reasons, not just ≥1 strict entry.
resolution_status_highlights.Md-1_coverage_count_discrepancy: fixed_as_specified — authoritative count 55/303 = 18.2% (Number.isInteger(parsed.queue_id) on each .json file). Tinker #1's 56/304 and hostile #2's 61/304 both off; disambiguated with 3 alternative counting methods.
resolution_status_highlights.Md-2_mixed_version_validator: fixed_as_specified — Step 7b validator timestamp-gated via QUEUE_ID_STRICT_AFTER env var (default 2026-04-20T00:00:00Z); WARN before cutoff, THROW after. Covers pre-patch-in-flight reviews.
resolution_status_highlights.Md-3_bypass_reason_unaudited: fixed_differently — moved bypass count threshold from 'daily-report generator' (no such query path) to tinker Mode 2 procedure (already walks shadow log). Pseudocode in migration_plan.tinker_mode_2_bypass_audit.
resolution_status_highlights.Md-4_history_cap_too_small: fixed_as_specified — bumped from 50 to 200 entries in block 1.
resolution_status_highlights.Mn-1: informational only per hostile reviewer's own tag; left as-is.
resolution_status_highlights.Mn-2_files_to_create_orphan: fixed_as_specified — instruction extended to git rm the seeded ISS file post-intake.
resolution_status_highlights.Mn-3_pushed_at_seconds_precision: declined — finding is polish, not correctness; evidence cited in disagreements_with_review.

suggested_fix.rationale: All 10 hostile-review findings addressed; 3 critical + block-7 + 4 major + 4 moderate fixed; 2 minors folded, 1 declined with evidence.

findings.description: Four tinker runs in one day (09:50, 10:13, 11:50, 13:05) — all operator-directed. Counter-argument from prior tinker (11:50Z): each has a distinct root cause (change-detection cooldown, pop-without-review, PROP-009 hostile review, PROP-009r hostile review). The cadence is justified by the adversarial refine loop, not a single issue re-opening four times.
findings.evidence: DIRECTIVE-20260418-001, -002, -003, -004 in monitor/tinker/operator-directives/. Each triggers a distinct hostile-review pass finding progressively narrower execution gaps. Hostile #2 found execution defects hostile #1 missed (shell-quote bug, ownership unregistered). Hostile #3 if commissioned would likely find proportionally less.

suggested_fix.rationale: Continue the adversarial loop as long as operator finds distinct execution gaps. If hostile #3 returns few findings, land PROP-009r2 as-is with the 3-commit apply order. Diminishing returns will show in review severity, not in request cadence.

findings.description: Lesson captured: always execute dry_run_check commands against the clone checkout END-TO-END, not just eyeball them. Hostile #2 block-7 shell-quote bug would have been caught by tinker #1's 11:50Z run if the dry_run_check commands had been run end-to-end.
findings.evidence: PROP-009r2 disagreements_with_review.C-1_escape_correctness: 'why_we_adopted_dry_run_check_anyway' acknowledges the pattern. PROP-009r2 format_constraints (per DIRECTIVE-004) makes end-to-end dry_run_check execution a HARD GATE for future revisions.

suggested_fix.rationale: Future-Mode-4 improvement: promote 'execute every dry_run_check end-to-end against the clone BEFORE declaring a PROP ready' to a mandatory Step in the Proposals module. Defer to a separate PROP (PROP-012) — out of scope for this run.

recommendations.description: Review and approve PROP-009r2. 3-commit apply order: (1) curmudgeon.md + build.js + workspace-sync.md atomic (step-1); (2) wait for first curmudgeon run; (3) decider.md. Flip enforce flag only after Mj-4 audit criteria met (majority strict_queue_id pops over 3-day window + ≥80% NEW-review queue_id + ≤3 distinct stuck tuples/day).
Mark PROP-009.status = 'superseded' and PROP-009r.status = 'superseded' explicitly. Prevents future greps from surfacing them as still-proposed.

Decide on hostile review #3 vs apply-as-is. Tinker's prior counter-argument: hostile #2 found execution gaps that hostile #1 missed; hostile #3 will find proportionally less. If operator wants extra insurance on the 3 critical fixes, run hostile #3 narrowly scoped to those items only — not a full re-read. Otherwise proceed to apply with Mod3 order.
PROP-010 for git→workspace reverse sync remains the most-deferred item. Today's FUSE staleness required repeated cross-checking against main HEAD. Next Mode 2 run should prioritize PROP-010 draft.

PROP-011 (multi-writer attention-inbox escalation) deferred from PROP-009 Mod2. Operator's DIRECTIVE-004 mentioned this as a natural next step. Tinker's call per directive — write if operator is focused on this area. RECOMMENDED: defer to tomorrow; 4 tinker runs today is already heavy.
PROP-012 to formalize 'always execute dry_run_check end-to-end' as a mandatory step in tinker-proposals-and-fixes.md. Captures today's block-7 lesson.

counter_arguments.position: PROP-009r2 is even heavier than r — 93KB vs 65KB — for a one-line substring-match bug.
counter_arguments.counter: Hostile review #2 found 3 critical execution gaps (flag ownership, cycle-finding wrong, block-7 shell bug). These are not theoretical — block-7 would have failed on first apply. The weight is an artifact of exhaustive fix documentation, not feature creep. Each section serves a purpose: resolution_status gates future greps; disagreements_with_review captures declined/alternative-fix reasoning; simulate-apply would catch apply-time drift. Over-engineering is cheaper than re-bug.

counter_arguments.position: C-2 factual disagreement risks looking defensive. If tinker gets two of three criticals right and argues the third, hostile #3 will push back harder.
counter_arguments.counter: The disagreement is concrete, not tone-based. PROP-009r2 provides a 9-line worked example with qids 100 and 101 showing exactly where the claim is written and how the next run's available-filter excludes the claimed review. If hostile #3 wants to rebut, they must rebut the worked example byte-for-byte. More importantly: tinker ALSO fixed the (weaker) concern by adding an explicit INVARIANT doc comment. Defense-in-depth, not defense-alone.

counter_arguments.position: Md-2 warn-then-throw with a hardcoded QUEUE_ID_STRICT_AFTER date is inelegant. An env-var cutoff date feels like a hack.
counter_arguments.counter: The alternative — waiting indefinitely for all pre-patch-in-flight reviews to drain — requires monitoring every curmudgeon invocation to know when drain is complete. The hardcoded date is a forcing function: 36h (cutoff is 2026-04-20T00:00:00Z vs step-1 commit ~2026-04-18) is enough time for every in-flight review to complete under even worst-case BAU. Operator can override the env var if needed; default is safe.

counter_arguments.position: 55/303 = 18.2% is a LOW starting point. Even with the Mj-4 reframe to NEW-review denominator, it will take weeks to accumulate 80% NEW-queue_id coverage at current churn.
counter_arguments.counter: Exactly why the shadow mode exists. 18.2% is what we start with; it grows monotonically from step-1 commit onward because curmudgeon's Step 7a/7b now REQUIRE queue_id on queue-sourced reviews. The denominator grows with the numerator (both are NEW reviews post-step-1). Under churn-and-burn, expect ≥80% within 3-5 days. Under BAU, ~2 weeks. Flip criterion is a real bar, not a trivial one — intentional.

counter_arguments.position: Fourth tinker directive in one day is overload. The shadow-mode apply could have been approved after r.
counter_arguments.counter: Each hostile pass found distinct execution defects. r had shell-quote bug (block 7), ownership unregistered (C-1), and factually-wrong ISS (C-3). Applying r as-is would have: (a) blocked step-1 commit validation on the shell-quote bug, (b) left the flag file unclassified with undefined direction-guard behavior, (c) filed a misleading ISS that would mis-direct a future fix. Four runs costs Opus context but saves applying a broken PROP. The real cost is operator time, not agent time.

notes_to_future_tinker: PROP-009r2 all 11 dry_run_checks verified end-to-end against /tmp/dmr-clone main HEAD 5f84f34 at 13:05Z. If operator applies and a check fails, cross-check whether main HEAD drifted between 13:05Z and apply time via git log --since.

The PROP-009r2 INVARIANT doc comment in block 1 (decider.md replace_lines) is load-bearing — any future edit to the Step E2 bash block must preserve it. A future tinker that 'cleans up comments' should NOT remove it.

Block 10 (build.js OWNERSHIP insert) and block 11 (workspace-sync.md OWNED_BY_GIT insert) are coupled — both must land in the same commit. If one lands without the other, workspace-sync direction-guard will fire on the flag file.
The Step 7b validator's QUEUE_ID_STRICT_AFTER env var defaults to 2026-04-20T00:00:00Z. If step-1 commit lands on or after 2026-04-19, bump this date to +36h from step-1 commit time.

Fresh audit count now authoritative at 55/303. If future tinker re-runs the count, use Number.isInteger on the top-level queue_id field, NOT grep text matches. The grep counts (60 and 71) include narrative text mentions inside nested strings.

The orthogonal finding (self-contradictory reviewed_at) is a genuinely separate bug from PROP-009r2's scope. The seeded ISS in files_to_create[0] captures it for decider intake. If the pattern persists after step-1 lands, consider a focused PROP on curmudgeon.md's reviewed_at generation in Step 7.
Operator-bypass count audit (Md-3) now lives in tinker Mode 2 procedure. Next Mode 2 run should implement the 3-line count-and-flag logic. No code change needed in decider.
If decider runs before PROP-009r2 applies, it will not see the new OWNERSHIP entry; the flag file does not exist yet. All behavior is identical to current until step-1 lands.
PROP-009.status and PROP-009r.status both remain 'proposed' in tracker until operator explicitly updates them. File an operator-facing recommendation.
Context cost this run: PROP-009r2 build + verify + simulate-apply + report consumed ~3200 lines. Under budget. Any 5th directive today should skip Mode 2/3 reference loads.
